SPANDANA SPHOORTY Q1: MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY 💬

• 📈 Management said the microfinance business is back on a growth trajectory, with improvement across key operating metrics

• 💰 PAT improved to ₹12 Cr from ₹5 Cr QoQ, while AUM grew 11% QoQ to ₹4,887 Cr

• 🚀 Disbursements remained strong at ₹1,371 Cr, with 61% of loans extended to new customers

• ✅ Collection efficiency improved to 99.5%, while PAR 30+ declined to 4.4% and GNPA improved 15 bps QoQ to 3.64%

• 💵 Recoveries from 90+ DPD customers stood at ₹51 Cr in Q1; cumulative recoveries reached ₹325 Cr over the last 18 months

• 🎯 Focus areas include expanding in underpenetrated markets, reviving weak branches, rolling out a new LOS platform and launching an individual loan product

• 🏦 Strong balance sheet with ₹1,316 Cr liquidity, 33.8% CRAR and ₹2,140 Cr net worth to support future growth

3i INFOTECH Q1 FY27 CONS NET PROFIT DOWN 14.4% AT ₹6.52 CR (YOY) vs ₹7.62 CR, DOWN 10.3% (QOQ) vs ₹7.27 CR

REVENUE UP 4.3% AT ₹177.94 CR (YOY) vs ₹170.55 CR, UP 1.2% (QOQ) vs ₹175.78 CR

EBITDA TURNAROUND AT ₹5.07 CR VS ₹-3.46 CR (YOY), TURNAROUND VS ₹-9.63 CR (QOQ)

MARGINS 2.85% V -2.03% (YOY), V -5.48% (QOQ)

**Vishal Mega Mart HSBC Brokerage Update**

**Rating & Target**
- Buy rating maintained.
- Target Price cut to ₹153.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Q1FY27 revenue in line with estimates.
- SSSG stood at 10%.
- Marginal EBITDA beat estimates.
- Gross margin improved.
- Lower discounting supported margins.

**Brokerage View**
- Discounting remained disciplined.
- Price hikes limited to select products.
- Higher-priced categories saw modest hikes.
- Management sees no further price hikes.

**Key Catalysts**
- Sustained double-digit SSSG.
- Gross margin improvement.
- Stable pricing strategy.

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC remains positive on Vishal Mega Mart, citing healthy same-store sales growth, improving gross margins through lower discounting, and a disciplined pricing strategy despite reducing its target price.**

**Stock Fundamental in detail                            
****@Fundamental3**

Spandana Sphoorty Financial | Q1 FY27 Results

Financial Performance
- AUM at ₹4,887 crore (-1% YoY, +11% QoQ).
- Disbursements ₹1,371 crore (+389% YoY, -11% QoQ).
- Income ₹303 crore (Flat YoY, +9% QoQ).
- NII ₹135 crore (+19% YoY, +39% QoQ).
- Impairment recorded ₹1 crore write-back.
- PPOP improved to ₹40 crore.
- PBT turned positive at ₹18 crore.
- PAT stood at ₹12 crore.

Operational Highlights
- NIM improved to 12.5%.
- ROA increased to 0.7%.
- ROE improved to 2.2%.
- GNPA reduced to 3.64%.
- NNPA declined to 0.68%.
- Gross collection efficiency reached 96.6%.
- Net collection efficiency improved to 95.9%.

Management Commentary
- Microfinance sector showing recovery.
- Business back on growth path.
- PAT and AUM improved sequentially.
- 61% of disbursements to new customers.
- X-bucket collection efficiency reached 99.5%.
- PAR 30+ declined to 4.4%.
- Recovered ₹51 crore from 90+ DPD accounts.
- Total recoveries reached ₹325 crore in 18 months.

Growth Initiatives
- Expand presence in underpenetrated markets.
- Revive weak-performing branches.
- Launch new Loan Origination System.
- Introduce individual loan products.
- Liquidity at ₹1,316 crore.
- CRAR at 33.8%.
- Net worth at ₹2,140 crore.

Impact
- Positive.
- Profitability returned.
- Asset quality improved.
- Collection efficiency strengthened.
- Growth momentum recovering.
- Strong capital supports expansion.

Key Takeaway
- Spandana Sphoorty delivered a strong turnaround in Q1 FY27 with a return to profitability, improving asset quality, higher collection efficiency and double-digit sequential AUM growth. Management believes the company has entered a recovery phase and is well positioned for sustainable growth backed by strong capital, liquidity and multiple business expansion initiatives.

**Brokerage Update**
- Nomura maintained **Buy** rating.
- Target price at **₹1,290**.

- FY27 margin guidance unchanged.
- EBIT margin guidance at **20–22%**.

**Management Update**
- CEO transition remains a key monitorable.
- Smooth succession execution in focus.

**Valuation View**
- Trading at attractive valuations.
- Valued at **13x FY28F EPS**.
- FY28F EPS estimated at **₹80.4**.

**Impact**
- **Positive**.
- Revenue outlook weakened.
- Margin guidance maintained.
- Attractive valuations support optimism.
- Buy rating retained.

**Key Takeaway**

Allied Blenders & Distillers | Q1FY27 Highlights

Financial Highlights (Consolidated)
- Revenue ₹984 crore (+5.8% YoY).
- EBITDA ₹120 crore (+1.2% YoY).
- EBITDA margin 12.2% (-60 bps YoY).
- PAT ₹45 crore (-18.7% YoY).
- One-off supply disruption impacted profits.

Adjusted Performance
- Adjusted EBITDA ₹144 crore (+21.4% YoY).
- Adjusted EBITDA margin 14.7% (+189 bps).
- Adjusted PAT ₹63 crore (+13.6% YoY).

Business Performance
- Revenue driven by premiumisation.
- Product mix continued improving.
- Volumes increased 6.2% YoY.
- Total volumes reached 9 million cases.
- P&A volumes grew 10.7% YoY.
- Mass Premium volumes grew 2.3% YoY.
- P&A value share rose to 59.3%.

Brand & Expansion
- ICONiQ White sold 3.1 million cases.
- ICONiQ White grew 33.8% YoY.
- Launched Zoya Pink.
- Presence expanded to 39 countries.
- ICONiQ entered 10 new markets.
- ABD Maestro expanded globally.

Management Outlook
- Premiumisation strategy progressing well.
- Investments continue in brands and distribution.
- India-UK FTA expected to boost margins.
- Medium-term topline growth targeted at mid-teens.

Impact
- Positive.
- Premium portfolio gaining momentum.
- International expansion accelerating.
- Temporary supply disruptions remain monitorable.

Key Takeaway
- Allied Blenders delivered another quarter of strong premiumisation-led growth, with higher volumes, improving product mix and rapid global expansion. While one-off supply chain disruptions affected reported profitability, the underlying business remains strong and management continues to target mid-teen revenue growth supported by premium brands and the India-UK FTA.

KRSNAA DIAGNOSTICS: EXPANDS HIMACHAL PRADESH PPP CT SCAN PROJECT TO 34 CENTRES 🏥📈

• 🤝 Krsnaa Diagnostics has signed a Public Private Partnership (PPP) agreement with the Health & Family Welfare Department, Government of Himachal Pradesh to set up, operate and manage CT Scan diagnostic facilities at 34 Government Health Institutions across the state. 

• 📈 The project scope has been expanded from 12 to 34 Government Health Institutions, making it one of the company's largest government partnerships for CT scan diagnostic services. 

• 📅 The project will run on a PPP model for an initial 7 years, with an option to extend by another 3 years, and will operate on a majority cash basis. 

• 💰 The company stated that the contract value cannot currently be estimated, as it is a new project. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The significant expansion in project scope strengthens Krsnaa Diagnostics' government healthcare portfolio, improves long-term revenue visibility and reinforces its leadership in PPP diagnostics.)

BELDING INDIA: ACQUIRES 100% STAKE IN EVOLVE IT SOLUTIONS 🚀🛡️

• 🤝 Belding India has acquired a 100% equity stake in Evolve IT Solutions Pvt. Ltd., strengthening its presence in advanced manufacturing and security technologies. 

• 🛡️ Evolve specializes in advanced X-ray inspection and security screening solutions, including baggage scanners, cargo inspection systems, vehicle scanners, under-vehicle surveillance systems and dual-view X-ray systems, serving airports, ports, defence, metro rail and other critical infrastructure. 

• ⚙️ The acquisition expands Belding's capabilities in AI-enabled imaging, industrial electronics, precision engineering and mission-critical security technologies, creating opportunities in both domestic and international markets. 

• 🇮🇳 The company said the acquisition supports its strategy to build an indigenous engineering and technology platform, while promoting Make in India, import substitution and long-term growth. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition broadens Belding's technology portfolio, strengthens its defence and security offerings, and enhances long-term growth prospects.)

Key takeaways from Nandan Kulkarni interview on the potential impact of US pharma tariffs.

While the market is focused on tariff headlines, Nandan argues that the bigger story is how tariffs could reshape the economics of the US generic drug industry.

Here are the insights that stood out to us 

1. The generic pricing model may finally reset
 - According to Nandan, US generic drug prices have been under pressure for years. If manufacturing shifts back to the US, the current pricing simply won't support the economics.
 - Higher labour costs, operating expenses, FDA site transfers, and fresh capital investment all require higher Average Selling Prices (ASPs) to make domestic manufacturing viable.
 - The industry may be entering a structural pricing reset rather than facing just another policy headwind.

2. Drug shortages could become a major catalyst
- One interesting point he highlighted is that shortages in several acute drug categories have already started appearing in the US.
 - Any attempt to accelerate onshoring could tighten supply further before new capacity comes online.
 - That potentially creates:
 • Better pricing
 • Improved profitability
 • Stronger negotiating power for reliable manufacturers
Sometimes the second-order effects matter more than the headline itself.

3. Existing US manufacturing becomes a competitive advantage
Not every company starts from the same position.
Nandan believes companies that already have manufacturing operations in the US—such as Sun Pharma, Lupin and Zydus Lifesciences—could adapt much faster if localisation gathers pace. Rather than building entirely new facilities, these companies may be able to expand existing sites and execute product transfers more efficiently.

4. The market may be underestimating the timeline
A key observation was that investors shouldn't wait for tariffs to be formally implemented.
The next 12–18 months could already see:
 • Pricing negotiations with PBMs
 • Strategic inventory build-up
 • Manufacturing transfer planning
 • Incremental US capex decisions
Markets often discount these developments well before policy becomes effective.

5. Execution—not tariffs—will separate winners
 - An important distinction Nandan made was between simple oral generics and complex biologics.
 - Traditional tablets and capsules are relatively easier to relocate.
 - Biosimilars and other complex products involve significantly longer technology transfers and regulatory approvals, making the transition far more challenging.

6. Better pricing could fund future growth
 - One concern investors have is whether companies will need massive balance sheet investments.
 - Nandan's argument is interesting: if generic prices improve because of tighter supply and higher manufacturing costs, part of the incremental profitability itself could finance future US manufacturing investments.
 - That changes the capital allocation equation.

7. The long-term opportunity extends beyond generics
Perhaps the most important takeaway was his long-term view.
Rather than remaining low-cost manufacturers, Indian pharma companies could accelerate investments in:
 - Complex generics
 - 505(b)(2) products
 - Specialty pharmaceuticals
 - Orphan drugs
 - Peptides
 - Innovation-led portfolios
If that transition plays out, tariffs may ultimately become a catalyst for higher-value pharmaceutical businesses rather than simply a near-term disruption.

Our takeaway
The discussion shifts the focus away from "Will tariffs hurt Indian pharma?" to a more important question:
Could tariffs accelerate a healthier industry structure with better pricing, stronger margins and greater innovation?

Infosys | Q1 FY27 Results

Financial Highlights
- Revenue ₹48,211 crore (+14% YoY, +4% QoQ).
- EBIT ₹10,163 crore (+30% YoY, +17% QoQ).
- EBIT Margin 21.1% (vs 20.8% YoY).
- PAT ₹7,769 crore (+12% YoY, -9% QoQ).
- CC Revenue Growth +1% QoQ.
- Dollar Revenue US$5.08 billion (+1% QoQ).

Deal Wins & Business Momentum
- TCV increased to US$3.6 billion (+13% QoQ).
- Strong large deal wins across segments.
- AI momentum translating into revenue.
- Continued market share gains.
- Infosys Topaz driving enterprise AI adoption.

Management Commentary
- AI demand gaining traction.
- Strategic partnerships with leading AI companies.
- Focus on employee AI reskilling.
- Continued investments in AI, talent and platforms.
- Improving productivity and operating leverage.

Guidance
- FY27 revenue growth guidance revised to 1.5%–3.0%.
- Earlier guidance was 1.5%–3.5%.
- Reflects a more cautious growth outlook.

Leadership Transition
- Ashiss Kumar Dash appointed CEO & MD-designate.
- To succeed Salil Parekh from 1 April 2027.
- Over 31 years with Infosys.
- Expected to lead the company's next AI-led growth phase.

Impact
- Neutral.
- Strong execution and deal pipeline.
- AI business continues to strengthen.
- Guidance cut may weigh on near-term sentiment.
- Leadership transition appears well planned.

Key Takeaway
- Infosys delivered healthy Q1 FY27 growth with stable margins, robust deal wins and increasing AI-led revenue momentum. However, the reduction in FY27 revenue guidance indicates a more cautious demand environment. While near-term sentiment may remain muted, the strong order book, continued AI investments and a well-planned leadership transition position the company well for long-term growth.

Jubilant Ingrevia | Q1 FY27 Results

Financial Highlights
- Revenue ₹1,300 crore (+25% YoY, +10% QoQ).
- EBITDA ₹209 crore (+36% YoY, +22% QoQ).
- EBITDA Margin 16% (vs 15% last year).
- PAT ₹106 crore (+41% YoY, +22% QoQ).
- EPS ₹6.7 (+41% YoY, +22% QoQ).

Business Performance
- Strong performance across all business segments.
- Broad-based revenue and profit growth.
- Healthy margin expansion.
- Better operational execution.

Key Monitorables
- Progress of the US$300 million agrochemical project.
- Customer execution and project timelines.
- Sustainability of volume growth.
- Contribution from pricing versus volumes.
- Management commentary on demand outlook.

Long-Term Growth Drivers
- Execution of the Pinnacle Strategy.
- Higher contribution from Specialty Chemicals.
- Expansion of CDMO business.
- Improving business mix towards value-added segments.
- Potential for stronger long-term profitability.

Impact
- Positive.
- Strong quarterly performance.
- Margin improvement continues.
- Execution remains the key value driver.
- Management commentary will determine earnings sustainability.

Key Takeaway
- Jubilant Ingrevia delivered a strong Q1 FY27 with healthy growth in revenue, profitability and margins across all business segments. While the quarterly performance is encouraging, the long-term investment case continues to depend on successful execution of the Pinnacle Strategy, progress on the US$300 million agrochemical project and the company's ability to increase the share of higher-margin Specialty Chemicals and CDMO businesses.

PRECISION WIRES: SILVASSA PLANTS PARTIALLY IMPACTED BY FLOODING 🌧️🏭

• 🌧️ Precision Wires said operations at its Silvassa plants have been partially impacted due to flooding and waterlogging caused by unprecedented heavy rainfall since July 21, 2026. Around 740 mm of rain was recorded in the last 24 hours, following 333 mm during the previous day. 

• 🔍 The company is assessing the extent of production loss and property damage, and the financial impact is yet to be quantified. 

• 🛡️ Precision Wires confirmed that it is adequately insured under an Industrial All Risk (IAR) insurance policy. 

• 🔄 The company expects operations to gradually normalize over the next few days, barring any unforeseen developments, while ensuring the safety of employees, equipment and materials.  

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Operations have been temporarily disrupted, but the impact is still being assessed, the assets are insured and the company expects a gradual normalization.)

VISHAL MEGA MART: TO CAP FOREIGN OWNERSHIP AT 49.99% 🇮🇳📊

• 🇮🇳 The Board has approved a proposal to cap aggregate foreign ownership at 49.99% of the company's total equity instruments (on a fully diluted basis), subject to shareholders' approval. The company will issue the required notice in due course.

• 🏢 The proposal has been made because one of the company's wholly owned subsidiaries is required to remain an Indian-owned and controlled company (IOCC) under applicable FEMA regulations.

• 📜 The company clarified that while it is currently Indian-owned and controlled, the cap is being introduced in line with SEBI's framework that allows listed companies to restrict aggregate FPI/non-resident investment with Board and shareholder approval.

• 👩‍💼 The Board also re-appointed Ms. Neha Bansal as a Non-Executive Independent Director for a second term of one year, from September 23, 2026 to September 22, 2027, subject to shareholders' approval.

🟢 Impact: Positive (The foreign ownership cap helps preserve the company's Indian-owned and controlled status, ensuring regulatory compliance and protecting operations of its subsidiary, while maintaining business continuity.)

PTC Industries has received a design & development order from ARDE (DRDO) for a Titanium Cradle used in the Indian Light Weight Tank programme.

Moving up the value chain! 

While the order value hasn't been disclosed due to the strategic nature of the project, the announcement is noteworthy for another reason.

Unlike conventional manufacturing contracts, this assignment requires PTC to design, develop, validate and manufacture a mission-critical titanium component.

This indicates a shift from executing customer-provided designs to participating earlier in the product development cycle, where engineering capability, material expertise and validation become equally important.

Titanium is increasingly being used in defence applications because of its high strength-to-weight ratio, corrosion resistance and durability, making it suitable for lightweight platforms such as the Indian Light Weight Tank.

The execution timeline for the project is 2.5 years, and further developments would depend on successful design validation and subsequent programme requirements.

PTC INDUSTRIES: BAGS DRDO DESIGN & DEVELOPMENT ORDER FOR INDIAN LIGHT WEIGHT TANK 🛡️🇮🇳

• 🛰️ Received a design & development order from ARDE, DRDO for a Titanium Cradle for the 105mm Indian Light Weight Tank

• 🚀 Marks PTC Industries' first fit-for-purpose design order, expanding beyond conventional build-to-print manufacturing

• ⚙️ Titanium Cradle is a mission-critical defence component supporting the tank's main gun barrel, breech and recoil mechanism

• 🏭 Strengthens PTC ONE™ ("From Melt to Mission") by adding indigenous design-led capability for advanced defence components

• 🇮🇳 Opens opportunities in lightweight titanium components for tanks, artillery systems and other next-generation defence platforms

🟢 Impact: Positive (Enhances PTC Industries' defence capabilities, moves up the value chain into design-led manufacturing and strengthens long-term growth opportunities.)

ZYDUS LIFESCIENCES: RECEIVES APPROVAL FOR PHASE III TRIAL OF DESIDUSTAT IN SICKLE CELL DISEASE 🧬🩺

• ✅ Received approval to conduct a Phase III clinical trial of Desidustat for treating anaemia in Sickle Cell Disease (SCD) in collaboration with ICMR

• 🧪 Approval follows a successful Phase II proof-of-concept study, which met its primary endpoint with positive efficacy and safety results

• 👥 The 203-day, multicentre, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled Phase III trial will enrol 164 patients

• 🌍 USFDA has granted Orphan Drug Designation (ODD) to Desidustat for Sickle Cell Disease and beta-thalassemia

• 🚀 Desidustat has the potential to become a first-in-class oral therapy for Sickle Cell Disease, subject to successful Phase III outcomes

🟢 Impact: Positive (Advances Zydus' innovative pipeline and strengthens long-term growth potential in rare disease therapies.)

@Concalls3

US Student Visa | Policy Update

Key Changes
- New rule effective September 15, 2026.
- "Duration of Status (D/S)" system ends.
- New entries capped at four years.
- Extensions require separate USCIS approval.
- Grace period reduced to 30 days.

For Current Students
- Students outside the US should return before Sept. 15.
- Those in the US by Sept. 15 can continue studies/OPT.
- Overall stay capped at four years.
- Stay cannot extend beyond November 2030.

Impact
- Affects F-1 and J-1 visa holders.
- Indian students among the most impacted.
- Universities advising early return.

Key Takeaway
- The proposed US student visa rule changes significantly alter stay and extension rules for F-1 and J-1 students. Eligible students currently outside the US may benefit from returning before September 15, 2026, while future entrants will face stricter time limits and approval requirements.

- Equity market share 7.38%.
- Fastest-growing Top-10 AMC YTD.
- Continued market share gains.

ETF & SIP Performance
- ETF QAAUM ₹2.43 lakh crore (+40% YoY).
- ETF market share 21.35%.
- Monthly systematic flows ₹11,030 crore.
- Annualised SIP book ₹44,600 crore.
- ETF contribution continued rising.

Investor Franchise
- Total folios reached 40.2 million.
- Added 0.7 million folios QoQ.
- Unique investors 24.1 million.
- Unique investor market share 39%.
- Retail AUM share 29%.
- Retail mix above industry average.

Impact
- Strong Positive.
- Healthy earnings growth.
- Strong AUM expansion.
- Continued market share gains.
- Industry-leading investor franchise.

Key Takeaway
- Nippon Life India AMC delivered another strong quarter with robust earnings growth, sustained market share gains and healthy expansion across ETFs and SIPs. Its industry-leading retail investor franchise and continued growth in AUM reinforce its strong competitive position in India's expanding mutual fund industry.

IndusInd Bank | Q1FY27 Results

Financial Highlights
- Loans ₹3,26,274 crore (-2% YoY, +3% QoQ).
- Deposits ₹4,14,766 crore (+4% YoY, +4% QoQ).
- NII ₹4,685 crore (+1% YoY, +7% QoQ).
- Other Income ₹1,787 crore (-17% YoY, +4% QoQ).
- Operating Profit ₹2,773 crore (+8% YoY, +21% QoQ).
- Provisions ₹1,384 crore (-21% YoY, -7% QoQ).
- Net Profit ₹1,037 crore (+72% YoY, +75% QoQ).

Business Performance
- Deposits continued steady growth.
- Loan book recovered sequentially.
- Funding franchise remained stable.
- Margin performance improved.

Profitability
- NIM improved to 3.57%.
- ROA increased to 0.78%.
- ROE improved to 6.26%.
- Lower provisions boosted profitability.

Asset Quality
- GNPA improved to 3.25%.
- NNPA declined to 0.95%.
- SMA 1 & SMA 2 reduced to 0.12%.
- Asset quality trends remained healthy.

Impact
- Positive.
- Strong profit recovery.
- Improving margins and asset quality.
- Lower credit costs supported earnings.
- Loan growth remains a key monitorable.

Key Takeaway
- IndusInd Bank delivered a strong Q1FY27 with sharp improvement in profitability driven by lower provisions, better margins and improving asset quality. While loan growth remained subdued on a YoY basis, sequential recovery in advances and a healthier balance sheet indicate improving business momentum.

UCO Bank | Q1FY27 Results

Financial Highlights
- Total Business ₹6,05,083 crore (+15.53% YoY, +2.50% QoQ).
- Total Deposits ₹3,32,315 crore (+11.28% YoY, +1.45% QoQ).
- Total Advances ₹2,72,768 crore (+21.18% YoY, +3.81% QoQ).
- Interest Income ₹6,996 crore (+8.70% YoY, +5.10% QoQ).
- NII ₹2,808 crore (+16.85% YoY, +7.42% QoQ).
- Total Operating Income ₹8,682 crore (+16.80% YoY, +17.88% QoQ).
- Operating Profit ₹2,810 crore (+79.84% YoY, +78.58% QoQ).
- PAT ₹656 crore (+8.07% YoY, -18.10% QoQ).

Business Performance
- Retail Advances ₹71,549 crore (+27.32% YoY).
- Agriculture Advances ₹38,952 crore (+30.01% YoY).
- MSME Advances ₹47,244 crore (+18.79% YoY).
- RAM portfolio ₹1,57,745 crore (+25.27% YoY).
- RAM share increased to 64.52%.
- CASA Ratio remained healthy at 36.94%.

Profitability
- NIM improved to 3.05%.
- Non-interest Income rose 69.10% YoY.
- Operating profit remained robust.
- Higher provisions impacted PAT growth.

Asset Quality
- GNPA improved to 2.08%.
- NNPA reduced to 0.25%.
- Slippage Ratio declined to 0.63%.
- Credit Cost improved to 0.39%.

Impact
- Positive.
- Strong loan growth across segments.
- Asset quality continued improving.
- Healthy operating performance.
- Higher provisioning weighed on earnings.

Key Takeaway
- UCO Bank delivered a healthy Q1FY27 with strong growth in advances, improving margins and continued improvement in asset quality. Robust growth in the high-yield RAM portfolio and lower slippages highlight strengthening fundamentals, although elevated provisions moderated PAT growth during the quarter.

Jefferies in IndiaMart
U-P, TP Rs 1650

Q1 revs were in line while margins/profits were ahead.

Decline in paid supplier base for third straight quarter was a key negative surprise.

Margin expansion was driven by lower customer acquisition costs, which should normalize once the gross addition picks up.

Continued weakness in sub adds can disrupt network effects historically enjoyed by the platform

Cut FY28-29E EPS by ~1.5-4%

Nomura on IndiaMart
Reduce, TP Rs 1810

Paying subscriber addition woes continue – reduction continued in 1Q

Product evolution critical to improve subscriber addition in medium to long term

Believe subscriber addition has to turn meaningfully positive for stock to rise significantly from here

Value Indiamart’s core business at 18x P/E and continue to assign a 1x book value to its recent investments.

**Nykaa | Brokerage Update (HSBC)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy (Upgraded).
- Target Price: ₹380.

**Investment Thesis**
- Sees Nykaa as online BPC leader.
- Gaining market share in fashion.
- Strong long-term growth visibility.

**Earnings Outlook**
- Raises FY28 EBITDA estimate by 12%.
- Driven by stronger BPC margins.
- Fashion business growth supports earnings.

**Long-Term Outlook**
- Growth visibility remains strong beyond FY30.

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC upgraded Nykaa to Buy, citing its leadership in online beauty & personal care, increasing fashion market share, improving profitability, and strong long-term growth prospects extending beyond FY30.**

**Bajaj Auto | Brokerage Update (MOSL)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy (Upgraded).
- Target Price: ₹12,096.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Earnings beat estimates.
- Strong margins drove outperformance.
- Outlook remains positive across segments.

**Management Commentary**
- Targets ~2.50 lakh monthly exports from Q2.
- Export volume ramp-up expected.

**Cash Flow**
- FCF reached ₹2,300 Cr in Q1.
- FCF doubled YoY.
- Cash conversion at ~80% of PAT.

**Key Takeaway**
- **MOSL upgraded Bajaj Auto to Buy, citing strong margin-led earnings, robust free cash flow generation, improving export outlook, and positive growth prospects across business segments.**

**Indian Hotels | Brokerage Update (Morgan Stanley)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Equal-weight.
- Target Price: ₹783.

**Business Outlook**
- Q1 growth momentum expected in Q2.
- FY27 outlook remains firm.
- Standalone RevPAR met expectations.

**Guidance**
- Maintains double-digit FY27 revenue growth guidance.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Morgan Stanley remains constructive on Indian Hotels, expecting growth momentum to continue into Q2 with a firm FY27 outlook, supported by stable RevPAR performance and management's confidence in delivering double-digit revenue growth.**

**TVS Motor | Brokerage Update (Macquarie)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Outperform.
- Target Price: ₹4,325.

**Q1 Assessment**
- PAT beat estimates.
- Lower credit costs supported earnings.
- Margins remained resilient.
- Asset quality improved.

**Key Monitor**
- Sustainability of current performance.
- Margin and asset quality trends.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie remains positive on TVS Motor, citing better-than-expected Q1 earnings, resilient margins, and stronger asset quality, while highlighting the sustainability of these improvements as the key factor to watch going forward.**

**Bandhan Bank | Brokerage Update (Macquarie)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Underperform.
- Target Price: ₹130.

**Q1 Assessment**
- PAT missed estimates.
- Margin outlook lowered.
- Credit costs remain elevated.

**Outlook**
- Recovery outlook remains uncertain.
- Earnings visibility remains weak.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie remains cautious on Bandhan Bank, citing weaker-than-expected earnings, pressure on margins, elevated credit costs, and an uncertain recovery trajectory, leading it to maintain an Underperform rating with a ₹130 target price.**

**M&M Financial Services | Morgan Stanley View**

**Brokerage View**
- **Equal-weight** maintained.
- Target price raised to **₹370**.
- Earlier target price **₹335**.
- Good Q1 performance.

**Q1 Highlights**
- PAT beat estimates by **7%**.
- Lower operating costs aided earnings.
- Lower credit costs supported profitability.
- Slightly higher NIM assumptions.

**Estimate Revisions**
- FY27–FY29 EPS raised by **8%**.
- Reflects improved cost outlook.
- Credit cost assumptions lowered.
- Margins expected to remain healthy.

**Valuation View**
- Higher target price assigned.
- Macro risks remain a consideration.
- Historical volatility acknowledged.
- Scope for valuation re-rating.

**Impact**
- **Positive**.
- Earnings outlook improved.
- Better profitability expectations.
- Valuation upside remains.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Morgan Stanley maintained its Equal-weight rating on M&M Financial Services while raising the target price to ₹370. A stronger-than-expected Q1, driven by lower operating and credit costs, prompted higher earnings estimates, with further re-rating potential despite macro and cyclical risks.**

text:
India paid an additional ₹2.02 trillion for crude oil imports in the AMJ 2026 quarter.

This was higher by 77%, despite volume dropping by 4.5%.

Axis has come up with a Buy report on 
Steel Strip Wheels (SSWL) with a target price of 305.

However, 2 things to note - 
1> Their estimates are petty conservative, management guidance is actually higher with a positive bias. So, EPS could very well be 10-15% higher. 

2> They have valued the company at 14 PE.
I am not sure why. If the company cracks the export market and is able to grow at 20%+ with margin upside, I am not sure if it will trade at such low valuations.

SO, the bigger opportunity here is the valuation rerating.
If market decides that it deserves a 25-30 PE which is still lower than most auto ancillary stocks, then this can be a huge winner. Lets see how this goes.

text:
Goldman Sachs initiates coverage on Jindal Stainless 
BUY rating and TP at ₹1,000 

Key reasons behind the bullish stance:
✅ Best placed to benefit from rising domestic stainless steel consumption
✅ Higher share of value-added products to support profitability
✅ Strong balance sheet provides headroom to nearly double capacity from the current 4.2 MTPA over the medium term
✅ Attractive valuations relative to expected RoE

A side note: Amit Dixit the author of the report has been constructive on Jindal Stainless since much lower price levels during his time at previous brokerage houses.

**Paytm | CLSA View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- EBITDA reported at **₹200 crore**.
- EBITDA marginally beat estimates.
- Payments GMV growth accelerated to **31% YoY**.
- Financial services revenue growth strengthened.

**Growth Drivers**
- Merchant payments gaining momentum.
- Financial services scaling well.
- Stable contribution margin at **55%**.
- Lower fixed costs boosted profitability.

**Key Monitorables**
- Payment take rate declined sequentially.
- Lower doubtful debt provisions aided EBITDA.
- Wallet license application submitted.
- Higher operating expenses expected ahead.

**Brokerage View**
- **CLSA:** **Outperform**.
- Target Price **₹1,050**.
- FY27–FY29 EBITDA estimates cut **2–3%**.
- Limited upside after recent stock rally.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA acknowledges Paytm's strong operational momentum in payments and financial services, but believes much of the optimism around a possible UPI MDR return is already reflected in the stock price. While profitability continues to improve, higher operating expenses could moderate earnings growth.**

**Paytm | Citi View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Revenue grew **28% YoY**.
- EBITDA reached **₹200 crore**.
- EBITDA beat estimates by **16%**.
- Contribution profit at **55%** of revenue.

**Growth Drivers**
- Higher merchant loan distribution.
- Lower cloud infrastructure costs.
- Merchant ecosystem expanding.
- Operating leverage improving.

**Key Monitorables**
- Payment margins impacted by device rentals.
- Potential UPI MDR implementation.
- MDR could improve payment margins.
- Earnings estimates revised upward.

**Brokerage View**
- **Citi:** **Buy**.
- Target Price raised to **₹1,560**.
- Estimates increased after Q1.
- FY28 EBITDA could rise **8–10%** if UPI MDR is introduced.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains bullish on Paytm, supported by strong operating performance, improving cost efficiency and robust merchant lending growth. A potential introduction of UPI MDR on large merchants could provide an additional boost to payment margins and future earnings.**

**Paytm | Goldman Sachs View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Strong quarterly performance.
- Revenue growth accelerated.
- EBITDA beat estimates by **20%**.
- Bonus issue plans deferred.

**Growth Drivers**
- Offline payment market share gains.
- Online merchant additions accelerated.
- Merchant loan distribution remained strong.
- Consumer postpaid business expanding.

**Business Outlook**
- Payment ecosystem gaining momentum.
- Merchant acquisition continues.
- Consumer lending supports growth.
- Profitability expected to improve sharply.

**Brokerage View**
- **Goldman Sachs:** **Buy**.
- Target Price **₹1,500**.
- FY27 revenue growth estimated at **27% YoY**.
- FY27 EBITDA expected to more than double.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Goldman Sachs remains positive on Paytm, driven by accelerating payments growth, sustained merchant lending traction and expanding consumer financial products. The brokerage expects strong revenue momentum and a sharp improvement in profitability through FY27.**

- Home care recorded biggest hikes.
- Personal care prices stayed volatile.
- Food inflation remained selective.
- Mixed pricing across FMCG categories.

- Britannia biscuits up **8%**.
- Coconut hair oil down **11%**.
- VAHO prices up **14%**.
- Pricing trends remained divergent.

**Sector Outlook**
- Home care pricing remains firm.
- Personal care volatility expected.
- Commodity movements remain key.
- FMCG pricing actions likely continue.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA's latest FMCG survey indicates pricing power is returning, with price increases significantly outnumbering cuts. Home and personal care remain the most volatile categories, while selective price hikes should support revenue growth for FMCG companies.**

ACCIDENT OF THE DAY : WAAREE, PREMIER ENERGIES 

*ALMM-II extension, -ve for Solar mfg, +ve for developers* 

 *What’s new?* 
- MNRE, on Saturday, announced limited extension to the implementation of ALMM List-II (Approved List of Models & Manufacturers for solar cells) for Net Metering and Open Access (OA) solar projects to Dec-26 (earlier: May-26). 
- The decision followed by industry concerns that domestic cell manufacturing capacity remains insufficient to meet near-term demand, potentially delaying projects and increasing costs.
- MNRE stated that the move is intended to facilitate a smoother transition towards domestic cell sourcing while protecting investments already made by developers and module manufacturers.
- Interestingly, the order specifically notes that the extension will also help module manufacturers monetize existing inventories and provide time for the domestic solar cell industry to scale up capacity before the full implementation of ALMM List-II.

 *Our view* 
 
1) Impact on Solar Module Manufacturers (Waaree Energies & Premier Energies)
Near-term impact: Negative
- This extension reduces the value creation opportunity for the manufacturers such as Waaree Energies and Premier Energies with stronger backward intergration
- As the industry manufacturing capacity expands and demand supply mismatch reduces over the next six months, the pricing power for early movers of cell capacity additon would erode for the DCR based modules
- On the non DCR module, there can be an increase in demand in Q2 and Q3, however, given the lower realization and margin compared to DCR modules, this would still be net negative on the FY27 financials of these companies.

2) *Impact on CleanMax* 
 *Near term impact: Positive* 
- For RE open access and rooftop developers like CleanMax, this could have a significant boost in the segment as there can be an increased demand from consumer to benefit from the capex cost savings
- For C&I developer, execution certainty is often more valuable than marginal equipment localization benefits, making this policy amendment an incremental positive for growth and project returns over the next six months.

 *3) Industry impact* 
- In our view, the strong solar capacity addition in FY26 was partly driven by capacity additions ramp up to benefit prior to the change in regulations.
- We earlier, therefore had anticipated the spurt of FY26 (44.6GW addition) to subside marginally in FY27 and estimated a 35GW addition for the year.
- However, the announced extension, we see upside risk to our estimate. BROKERAGE NOTE

- Jio compounding continues.

- Retail EBITDA upside likely.
- Execution remains healthy.

**Retail Outlook**
- EBITDA to double in three years.
- Management reiterated target.
- Margin recovery expected.

**Brokerage View**
- **Macquarie:** Outperform, TP **₹1,510**.
- Positive on Jio.
- Constructive on Retail.
- Earnings upside possible.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie sees upside to consensus driven by sustained Jio growth, stronger digital services and improving retail EBITDA, despite near-term retail margin pressure.**

**Reliance Industries | Q1FY27 CLSA Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA beat estimates **3%**.
- PAT beat estimates **20%**.
- O2C outperformed expectations.
- Higher other income supported.

**Business Performance**
- Retail missed estimates.
- Interest costs increased.
- FMCG growth remained strong.
- Media momentum continued.

**Retail Outlook**
- Retail growth subdued FY27.
- EBITDA to double in three years.
- Long-term outlook positive.

**Growth Triggers**
- New energy ramp-up.
- O2C strength continues.
- Retail KPIs improving.
- Media & FMCG value unlocking.

**Brokerage View**
- **CLSA:** Outperform, TP **₹1,800**.
- Positive on long-term growth.
- Multiple catalysts ahead.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA remains positive on Reliance as strong O2C performance, resilient FMCG and media growth, retail EBITDA expansion and new energy ramp-up provide multiple long-term growth triggers.**

**Reliance Industries | Q1FY27 Nomura Update**

**O2C Business**
- EBITDA **₹17,000 crore**.
- Up **17% QoQ**.
- Four-year high.
- Crack spreads boosted earnings.

**E&P Business**
- EBITDA **₹5,000 crore**.
- Up **19% QoQ**.
- Strong recovery.

**Jio Telecom**
- Revenue **₹34,200 crore**.
- Up **2.5% QoQ**.
- Subscriber growth continued.
- ARPU improved.

**Retail Business**
- Revenue **₹90,400 crore**.
- Missed estimates **4%**.
- Down **8% QoQ**.
- Margin recovery key.

**Brokerage View**
- **Nomura:** Buy, TP **₹1,690**.
- Energy businesses outperformed.
- Retail remains watchpoint.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Strong energy-led quarter with record O2C earnings, improving E&P, steady Jio growth and retail margin recovery remaining the key monitorable.**

**Reliance Industries | Q1FY27 GS Update**

**O2C Business**
- Core EBITDA broadly in line.
- O2C EBITDA up **17% QoQ**.
- Performance beat market expectations.
- Outlook improves for Q2FY27.

**O2C Drivers**
- Higher crude premiums impacted.
- Logistics costs remained elevated.
- Fuel marketing under-recoveries.
- Windfall taxes weighed.
- Planned refinery turnaround impacted.

**Retail Business**
- EBITDA margin down **80 bps YoY**.
- Higher digital commerce investments.
- Hyperlocal delivery expansion continued.
- Margins faced near-term pressure.

**New Energy**
- Solar PV plant nearing commissioning.
- Battery manufacturing progressing.
- Integrated manufacturing platform advancing.
- New energy execution on track.

**Brokerage View**
- **Goldman Sachs:** Buy, TP **₹1,870**.
- Core performance met expectations.
- O2C outlook turning constructive.
- Positive long-term investment view.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Reliance delivered a steady quarter with resilient O2C performance, improving outlook for refining, continued investments in retail and meaningful progress in its new energy business. Goldman Sachs remains positive with a Buy rating and ₹1,870 target price.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Adjusted EBITDA/ton **₹14,990**.
- Up from **₹12,232 QoQ**.
- Operating profitability improved.
- Earnings remained strong.

**Balance Sheet**
- Net debt **₹46,157 crore**.
- Down from **₹53,870 crore**.
- Leverage reduced significantly.
- Balance sheet strengthened.

**Overseas Operations**
- US EBITDA **US$15.9 million**.
- Italy EBITDA **€7.1 million**.
- Overseas business turned profitable.
- Operational performance improved.

**Brokerage Views**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹1,650**.
- **Motilal Oswal:** Buy, TP **₹1,470**.
- **Nuvama:** Reduce, TP **₹1,172**.
- Consensus remains constructive.

**Growth Outlook**
- Capacity target **80 MT** by FY32.
- EPS expected to treble FY26–FY29.
- Margin outlook remains healthy.
- Long-term expansion continues.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JSW Steel delivered a strong quarter with higher EBITDA per tonne, sharp debt reduction and a turnaround in overseas operations. While brokerages differ on valuation, most remain positive on the company's long-term earnings growth, capacity expansion and improving profitability.**
